text: Micro820 Pin 1 (TXD+) wires to GS10 Pin 3 (S+) which is RS-485 A. Micro820 Pin 2 (TXD-) wires to GS10 Pin 4 (S-) which is RS-485 B. Micro820 Pin 5 (COM) wires to GS10 Pin 5 (SG) signal ground. Use shielded twisted pair (Cat5e STP). Install 120 ohm termination resistor across S+ and S- at the VFD end if cable run exceeds 30 ft. Route RS-485 cable away from VFD output power cables.
P09.00 baud rate set to 1 = 9600. P09.01 slave address must match PLC target node (both = 1 by default). Both sides must use 9600 baud 8 data bits no parity 2 stop bits (9600/8N2).

If no Modbus response: A/B polarity swapped, swap S+ and S- at PLC terminal block. CRC errors mean baud mismatch — verify both sides 9600/8N2. Timeout errors mean wrong slave address — VFD P09.01 must equal PLC target node. If VFD ignores commands, P00.04 must be set to 2 (RS-485 run source). Intermittent comms means EMI from power cables — reroute RS-485 away from VFD output.
P00.04 = 2 sets the run source to RS-485. Required to allow Modbus commands to control the drive instead of the keypad.
